text.title: Async Standup Methodology Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Async Standup Methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Standup template is exactly 3 fields: yesterday / today / blockers. No extra fields, no narrative paragraph.
rule.rationale: Bounded structure makes scanning <60s/team; narrative collapses to vibes within 2 weeks.

rule.statement: Each participant posts within a fixed window (e.g. by 11:00 local). Late posts roll to next day.
rule.rationale: Without window, post stream is unread; window creates the digest moment.

rule.statement: Any 'blockers' field non-empty triggers a named owner + ETA within 24h. Otherwise blocker is theatre.
rule.rationale: Blocker visibility without resolution = noise + learned helplessness.

rule.statement: Weekly: aggregate blockers, count by cause (waiting / unclear / capacity), surface top 3.
rule.rationale: Pattern in blocker causes is the signal; daily view obscures it.

rule.statement: Daily standups archive into a queryable log (channel or Notion DB), not deleted. Required for blocker rollup + onboarding.
rule.rationale: Without archive, weekly rollup is impossible; new hires can't reconstruct context.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-async-standup-methodology.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
